market_context: US stock futures for Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq 100 are rallying over 2% pre-market after President Trump delayed strikes on Iranian infrastructure by two weeks and announced a potential ceasefire with a 10-point negotiation proposal from Iran[1]. Yesterday's close saw Dow down 0.18% at 46585, amid mixed sector performance and ongoing geopolitical tensions[1]. Earnings season begins with banks, alongside steady consumer spending but concerns over growth, labor volatility, and elevated inflation forecasts[2][3][5][6].

predictions.reasoning: US500 index at 6801 points up 2.78% today, with S&P 500 futures over 2% higher on Trump's Iran ceasefire delay and negotiation proposal[1]. Yesterday's US500 close at 6772 supports upward momentum into close[1]. Geopolitical de-escalation typically boosts broad market indices like SPY.

Nasdaq 100 futures up more than 2% pre-market amid risk-on sentiment from Iran truce delay[1]. Despite Q1 tech sector weakness (-7.57%), today's news overrides recent pressures[2]. Broader futures rally supports Nasdaq-heavy QQQ.

Dow futures up over 2% after yesterday's 0.18% decline to 46585, driven by Trump ceasefire announcement[1]. Energy and industrials showed Q1 strength (+37.91% and +4.55%), aligning with de-escalation benefits[2]. Pre-market gains likely extend through session.

Geopolitical de-escalation in Middle East reduces oil supply disruption fears after Hormuz Strait tensions[1][4]. Energy sector led Q1 with +37.91% gains amid prior risks[2]. Futures rally amplifies sector upside.
